§6Artificial Xen Crystal & Ghiorsium§f

§aTwo exotic endgame materials with unique applications in lasers and nuclear fuels!§f

§6Artificial Xen Crystal§f
An §bArtificial Xen Crystal§f (hbm:crystal_xen) is a synthetic crystal grown in a controlled environment using exotic materials including §bEuphemium§f. It has unique optical properties useful for advanced laser systems.

§6Production§f:
§b• Requires euphemium as a key ingredient
§b• Produced through specific chemical processes

§6Uses§f:
§b• §bAdvanced Laser Systems§f — the crystal's optical properties make it ideal for high-powered lasers
§b• §bSpecialized Reactor Components§f

§6Ghiorsium-336§f
§bGhiorsium-336§f (Gh-336) is an exotic trans-schrabidic element that represents one of the heaviest artificially produced elements. It exists in billet (hbm:billet_gh336), cladding (hbm:cladding_ghiorsium), and insert (hbm:insert_ghiorsium) forms.

§6Uses§f:
§b• §bGhiorsium Cladding§f — used in advanced nuclear fuel elements
§b• §bGhiorsium Insert§f — specialized reactor component
§b• §bGhiorsium-336 Billets§f — for advanced fuel processing

§eTip§f: Both of these materials are produced relatively late in progression. Ghiorsium serves primarily as an advanced fuel component, while the Xen Crystal is more specialized for laser-related applications.
